title: Satyrium cornutum
taxonomy: Satyrium cornutum is classified within the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the phylum Ascomycota. Ascomycota is a large and diverse group of fungi, including cup fungi, mushrooms, and lichens. Satyrium cornutum further belongs to the class Ascomycetes, which encompasses a wide range of fungi with different characteristics and growth habits. Within the class Ascomycetes, the order Helotiales is where Satyrium cornutum is placed, characterized by small to medium-sized fungi with intricate fruiting bodies. The family Helotiaceae is the final classification level for Satyrium cornutum, encompassing a variety of fungi with similar characteristics.

intro: Satyrium cornutum, commonly known as Cape Bee Orchid or Yellow Bee Orchid, is a plant species within the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and acting as an antimicrobial agent. The bioactive constituents of Satyrium cornutum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Satyrium cornutum include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
